summary: Matched and linked the common fighter damage-fall source file by making a small local-variable adjustment in ftCo_DamageFall.c and switching the object from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The functional behavior appears unchanged: ftCo_80090594 now copies the FtMotionId parameter msid into a local motion_id before passing it to calcShift, which was sufficient for codegen matching.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageFall.c is built as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
In ftCo_80090594, introduced a local FtMotionId motion_id initialized from the msid parameter.
Changed the calcShift call from calcShift(hitlag_mul, msid, dmg) to calcShift(hitlag_mul, motion_id, dmg).
No PR body, comments, or review discussion were present in the provided slice.
smart_moves: Used a minimal source-level codegen nudge rather than restructuring logic: the only behavioral code change was introducing a local alias for msid.
Kept the matching proof explicit by updating configure.py to link ftCo_DamageFall.c as Matching once the function/codegen issue was resolved.
Preserved existing naming style for fighter motion IDs by using motion_id, matching nearby field and parameter naming conventions such as ground_or_air and hitlag_mul.
decomp_lessons: A parameter-to-local copy can affect register allocation or instruction scheduling enough to match, even when the C-level behavior is equivalent.
When a file becomes matching, update the build configuration from NonMatching to Matching in the same PR so link status remains synchronized with source changes.
For small matching-only changes, avoid unnecessary semantic edits; keep the diff narrow and easy to audit.
naming_conventions: FtMotionId is used for fighter motion-state identifiers.
Local variable motion_id uses snake_case and describes the FtMotionId value more explicitly than the original parameter name msid.
Existing fighter damage fields use names such as fp->dmg.x18fa_model_shift_frames and fp->dmg.x18FC; this PR did not rename those fields.
Function names in this area include generated/address-style names such as ftCo_80090594 when a better semantic name is not yet established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduce a typed local alias, FtMotionId motion_id = msid, before later use to influence generated code without changing behavior.
Pass the local alias to a helper call, calcShift(hitlag_mul, motion_id, dmg), instead of passing the original parameter directly.
Use configure.py object status changes as the repository-level signal that a translation unit now matches.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs where a function matched by copying a parameter into a local variable before a call.
Search for remaining NonMatching ftCommon damage-related files such as ftCo_Damage.c and ftCo_DamageIce.c.
Inspect ftCo_80090594 and calcShift in later history for semantic renaming opportunities once behavior is better understood.
